perTest.details: Network-free smoke of build_user_rs_request against the shipped build: package-root export; plain-vs-{'value':...} field asymmetry encoded exactly once via the module-level _RS_REQUEST_WRAPPED_FIELDS frozenset (owner plain; format/citation_type/pickup_location wrapped; pickup_location_type plain); int->str year coercion; agree_to_copyright_terms defaults to bool True; 'extra' escape hatch wraps plain strings for wrapped fields, passes through already-wrapped dicts, leaves plain fields plain; required-argument guards on owner/format/citation_type raise AlmaValidationError; inspect confirms Users.create_user_rs_request keeps request_data as required positional with keyword-only validate defaulting False. Pure function under test; no HTTP issued, no SANDBOX state touched. 1 passed in 0.01s.

Live SANDBOX round-trip: a body produced entirely by build_user_rs_request was ACCEPTED by Alma on a real create (build -> create -> GET; request_id round-tripped, printed as 'LEFT-IN-PLACE request_id=<request_id>'). No '[almaapitk hint:' appeared, supplying the positive no-behavior-change evidence for issue #194. NOTE: this is a re-run under an amended prescription (2026-07-22) — the original format PHYSICAL + citation_type BOOK shape reproducibly hit an Alma SANDBOX HTTP 500 (archived as t-197-2.attempt1/attempt2 logs, superseded); the test now uses the live-proven DIGITAL + citation_type CR shape, with journal_title and year added since they are mandatory for digital articles (Alma error 401930). A failure would therefore indict the builder's shape, not a code-table guess. OPERATOR OVERRIDE (standing): cleanup deliberately waived — the created request is intentionally left in SANDBOX for inspection; no DELETE/cancel code exists in the test. Absence of cleanup is not a failure. 1 passed in 3.49s.

Network-free smoke of opt-in soft validation (issue #194 option 2): validate=True on Users.create_user_rs_request short-circuits BEFORE any HTTP request, raising AlmaValidationError that names the offending field for wrong-table codes on format (the 'P' purchase-request code from the issue reproduction), pickup_location_type, and citation_type. Also pinned: deliberate permissiveness on citation_type (BK/CR/BOOK/JOURNAL all pass); both encodings accepted (wrapped dict and bare string); non-string values skipped; only the three code-table fields inspected; validate is keyword-only and defaults False; docstring cheat sheet present (option 3). The '<user_primary_id>' placeholder is deliberately nonexistent so any leaked network call would fail loudly. No SANDBOX state read or mutated. 1 passed in 0.01s.

Live reproduction of issue #194: POSTed the known-good RS-borrowing body with format deliberately set to wrong-code-table value 'P' (validate= intentionally NOT passed, proving validation is off by default and the request reached Alma). Alma rejected with HTTP 400 and the mangled dead-end message shape reproduced (mangled_shape=True); the client raised AlmaAPIError with an appended '[almaapitk hint: ...]' that names 'format' with its PHYSICAL/DIGITAL values and points at build_user_rs_request and validate=True; the 40166411 collision did not misclassify it as a POL error; tracking_id attribute intact (hint is message-only). No request was created (the create was rejected), so nothing was left in SANDBOX by this test; OPERATOR OVERRIDE (standing) waives cleanup regardless. 1 passed in 0.43s.

Network-free smoke pinning error-surfacing internals against the shipped build: the mangled-message detector and endpoint-scoped hint selection (RS-borrowing hint on the resource-sharing surface, generic hint elsewhere); a well-rendered Alma error that names its field is left untouched; unrelated messages pass through unchanged; the 40166411 code collision resolved endpoint-scoped (AlmaInvalidPolModeError meaning kept on acquisitions by default, plain AlmaAPIError on the RS surface); 401890 mapped to AlmaResourceNotFoundError despite arriving as HTTP 400. All calls are pure functions over strings; no HTTP issued, no SANDBOX state touched. 1 passed in 0.01s.
